revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Jonozuka Kofun]][[en:Jōnozuka Kofun]][[ja:上ノ塚古墳]][[zh:上之塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Jonozuka Kofun]][[en:Jōnozuka Kofun]][[ja:上ノ塚古墳]][[zh:上之塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Jonozuka Kofun]][[en:Jōnozuka Kofun]][[ja:上ノ塚古墳]][[zh:上之塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōnozuka Kofun]][[ja:上ノ塚古墳]][[zh:上之塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Jōnozuka Kofun | native_name = 上ノ塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Jonozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Jōnozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Jōnozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.468056|N|135.8745|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|qid=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th to 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Jōnozuka Kofun'''|上ノ塚古墳| Jōnozuka Kofun}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Wakibukuro neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Wakasa, Fukui|en|Wakasa, Fukui|qid=Q1349039|lt=Wakasa, Fukui|1=Wakasa, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1935.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138447 |title= 上ノ塚古墳 |trans-title= Jōnozuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the largest {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in the Wakasa region and was built from the end of the 4th century to the early 5th century AD.
==Overview== The Jōnozuka Kofun is one of a group of seven ''kofun'' located in central Wakasa at the western foot of Mount Zenbu. It is located west of the tracks of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|qid=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}} railway and is in the center of the group. Together with the [[Nishizuka Kofun]] and the [[Nakatsuka Kofun]], it was known to local legend as the tomb of one of the "Kings of Wakasa". The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The entire length is about 100 meters in three tiers, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 64 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion at 48 meters with a length of 36 meters. It is orientated to the north. It has retained most of its original shape, and the existence of the former moat can be confirmed. The round portion of the tumulus has a height of six meters and was covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' of various types have been found in the surrounding fields.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Currently, the site of the moat is under [[pa11y field|rice pa11ies]] and the tumulus is surrounded by farmland. The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1992, and in 2008 by {{ill|Ground-penetrating radar|en|Ground-penetrating radar|qid=Q503560|lt=Ground-penetrating radar|1=Ground-penetrating radar}}.The {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} discovered, mostly pieces of wood and metal, included items of Korean and Chinese origin, and are displayed at the Wakasa Museum of History and Culture. The name of the person interred is unknown; however from the name of the nearby hill (Zenbuyama, 膳部山), it mostly likely corresponds to the grave of a head of the Kashiwade clan (膳氏), who are recorded in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as having served as ''[[Kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Wakasa Province|en|Wakasa Province|qid=Q1053713|lt=Wakasa Province|1=Wakasa Province}} since the time of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kaminaka Station|en|Kaminaka Station|qid=Q11358411|lt=Kaminaka Station|1=Kaminaka Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Obama Line|en|Obama Line|qid=Q1058227|lt=Obama Line|1=Obama Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;" |+ Jōnozuka Kofun Cluster |- ! name !! type !! Length !! date |- | Jōnozuka Kofun || keyhole || 100m || early 5th century |- | Shiroyama Kofun || keyhole || 63m || mid 5th century |- | [[Nishizuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 74m || end 5th century |- | [[Nakatsuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 72m || late 5th century |- | Jūsen-no-mori Kofun || keyhole || 68m || early 6th century |- | [[Kamifuna Tsuka Kofun|Kamifunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 70m || early 6th century |- | [[Shimofuna Tsuka Kofun|Shimofunazuka Kofun]] || keyhole || 85m || mid 6th century |}
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|qid=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== [[Category:Jonozuka Kofun}} *[http://www1.city.obama.fukui.jp/japan_heritage/bunkazai/detail.php?lang=EN Obama city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/wakasacho-jonozuka.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
